content:
# CLI - Command Line Interface Reference

## TUI Mode

Run `opencode` to start the terminal UI.

### Flags

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--continue` | Continue from the last session |
| `--session <id>` | Continue a specific session |
| `--fork <id>` | Fork a session from a specific point |
| `--prompt <text>` | Start with an initial prompt |
| `--model <model>` | Specify the model to use |
| `--agent <agent>` | Specify the agent to use |
| `--port <port>` | Set the server port |
| `--hostname <hostname>` | Set the server hostname |

## Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `agent create` | Create a new agent |
| `agent list` | List available agents |
| `attach` | Attach to a running OpenCode session |
| `auth login` | Log in to OpenCode |
| `auth list` | List authenticated accounts |
| `auth logout` | Log out |
| `github install` | Install GitHub integration |
| `github run` | Run GitHub integration |
| `mcp add` | Add an MCP server |
| `mcp list` | List MCP servers |
| `mcp auth` | Authenticate with an MCP server |
| `mcp logout` | Log out from an MCP server |
| `mcp debug` | Debug MCP server connections |
| `models --refresh` | Refresh the model list |
| `run` | Run a non-interactive command |
| `serve` | Start OpenCode in server mode |
| `session list` | List sessions |
| `stats` | Show usage statistics |
| `export` | Export sessions |
| `import` | Import sessions |
| `web` | Start the web interface |
| `acp` | ACP (Agent Client Protocol) commands |
| `uninstall` | Uninstall OpenCode |
| `upgrade` | Upgrade OpenCode |

## Global Flags

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--help` | Show help |
| `--version` | Show version |
| `--print-logs` | Print logs to stdout |
| `--log-level <level>` | Set log level (debug, info, warn, error) |

## Environment Variables

- `OPENCODE_AUTO_SHARE` - Automatically share sessions
- `OPENCODE_CONFIG` - Path to config file
- `OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR` - Path to config directory
- Various other environment variables for configuration

## Experimental Env Vars

OpenCode supports experimental environment variables for features that are still in development. These are documented in the release notes and changelog.
